def: adj. (f. -ī), Unctuous, oily, greasy, fat, rich; smooth, glossy, sleek; polished, clean, shining: slippery; slimy; emollient, bland, mild, soft; smooth-faced, smooth-spoken, plausible, specious; — beautiful; showy, gay; incontinent, lewd, wanton; — ćiknā, s.m. Oil, grease, fat
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Oily, greasy; smooth-spoken, plausible; humorous, amusing; glossy, sleek; gay, decked out; — ćiknī-cupṛī bāteṅ banānā or karnā, To use oily speech, to speak plausibly, to butter, to gammon, wheedle, flatter
To be always decked out, to wear showy clothes
ćiknī ḍalī, s.f. = ćiknī supārī, s.f. Betel-nut boiled in milk (and so prepared to be cut up and eaten with betel-leaf or pān)
v.t. To grease, to lubricate; to smooth, to polish

s.m. 'A greasy vessel'; one who is deaf to reason or admonition, an incorrigible person, a shameless person; an incontinent person, a profligate; — ćiknā ghaṛā bannā, To be or become incorrigible, &c.
s.f. Clay, potter's earth; loamy or clayey soil.

def: s.f. The betel-nut.
s.f. = ćīkaṛ, and kīćaṛ, qq.v.
v.n. = ćikārnā, q.v.
s.m. Barley meal.
adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, viscous, gummy, glutinous, tenacious; clammy; clotted; covered with grease and dirt, greasy and black; dirty, filthy ( = ćīkaṭ; cf. ćīk, and kīć).
adj. & s.m. = ćikin, q.v.
s.f. A rotten betel-nut.
adj. & s.m. Flat-nosed; — one who has a flat nose.

adj. (f. -ī), Unctuous, oily, greasy, fat, rich; smooth, glossy, sleek; polished, clean, shining: slippery; slimy; emollient, bland, mild, soft; smooth-faced, smooth-spoken, plausible, specious; — beautiful; showy, gay; incontinent, lewd, wanton; — ćiknā, s.m. Oil, grease, fat
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Oily, greasy; smooth-spoken, plausible; humorous, amusing; glossy, sleek; gay, decked out; — ćiknī-cupṛī bāteṅ banānā or karnā, To use oily speech, to speak plausibly, to butter, to gammon, wheedle, flatter
To be always decked out, to wear showy clothes
ćiknī ḍalī, s.f. = ćiknī supārī, s.f. Betel-nut boiled in milk (and so prepared to be cut up and eaten with betel-leaf or pān)
v.t. To grease, to lubricate; to smooth, to polish

s.m. 'A greasy vessel'; one who is deaf to reason or admonition, an incorrigible person, a shameless person; an incontinent person, a profligate; — ćiknā ghaṛā bannā, To be or become incorrigible, &c.
s.f. Clay, potter's earth; loamy or clayey soil.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀡 (cikkaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चिक्कण (cikkaṇa).

def: s.f. Oiliness, greasiness, fatness; sleekness; smoothness; glossiness, gloss; cleanness, polish; beauty.
s.f. Oil, grease, fat, butter; greasinoss, &c. ( = ćiknāhaṭ, q.v.); fat of meat; — incontinence, wantonness.

ety: ćiknā˚ = Prk. चिक्कणाव(इ), or चिक्कणावे(इ) = S. चिक्कण (or चिक्किण)+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To oil, grease, to lubricate; to smooth, polish, clean ( = ćiknā karnā); v.n. To grow fat, become plump, become sleek.
s.f. = ćiknāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = ćiknāwaṭ, q.v.
s.f. A loamy or clayey soil; rich and highly cultivated land.
s.f. A loamy or clayey soil; rich and highly cultivated land.
v.t. = ćiknānā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Restless, inconstant.
v.t. To peck.
s.f. A kind of deal wood (cf. ćīr).
s.m. = ćiksā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of pulvil or sweet-scented powder of many ingredients; — a small earthen lamp (syn. ćughṛā; cf. ćirāg̠).
s.m. An inferior kind of silk or ṭasar cloth.
adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, viscous, gummy, glutinous, tenacious; clammy; clotted; covered with grease and dirt, greasy and black; dirty, filthy ( = ćīkaṭ; cf. ćīk, and kīć).
adj. (f. -ī), Greasy; filthy, &c. ( = ćikkaṭ, q.v.); — s.m. An oil-merchant
s.f. Clayey soil ( = ćīkaṭ; cf. ćikkaṭ and ćikṭā).
s.f. A sticky or tenacious soil, a clayey soil.
s.m. The hair of the head; a lock of hair.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चक्र (cakra). Doublet of چرخہ (carxa), چاک (cāk), چکر (cakr), and چَکَّہ (cakka).

def: adj. Inconsiderate, rash; rashly criminal, punishing or injuring others without consideration.
s.f. (dialec.) = ćīr or ćīṛ, q.v.
s.f. A kite, Falco cheela
subs: s.m. 'Swoop of a kite'; a boy's game (something like the game of bear-baiting)
To pounce upon in the manner of a kite, to snatch
adj. & s.m. (colloq.), Having long shanks; — long-shanks
s.m. (colloq.), 'The young of a kite'; a stupid or foolish person.

ety: contrac. of ćihal or ćihil, q.v.
def: adj. Forty.
s.m. A fried cake of pulse-meal ( = ćilṛā).
adj. (f. -ī), Muddy, miry, splashy, slimy.
s.f. Brilliance, glitter, refulgence.
v.n. To squirt; to emit sperm (as mares).
v.n. To shine, sparkle, glitter.
adj. (f. -ī), Bright, shining, glittering.
s.f. The part of a ḥuqqa which holds the tobacco and the fire
subs: s.m. The servant who prepares the ćilam
s.f. The work or office of a ćilam-bardār; (fig.) menial or low service
s.m. Tobacco enough for one ćilam; a little tobacco
s.m. A great or excessive smoker
v.n. To fill or prepare a ćilam; — to serve or wait (upon).

def: s.f. The part of a ḥuqqa fixed under the ćilam.
s.f. A wash-hand-basin of metal (with or without a cover; syn. silafćī).
s.f. = ćillaṛ, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. ćil, 'cry.'
s.f. Talc (syn. abrak).
v.n. To scream, keep on screaming, to shriek, to screech.
v.n. To be very hot (as the sun).
adj. (f. -ī), Hot, burning, scorching (as the sun).

ety: S. क्षीर or शरल+गोद, H. ćīṛ+gūda
def: s.m. The seed or kernel of the Pinus gerardiana; the cone of this pine; the tree itself. (The word is derived by native lexicographers from ćil = ćihal, 'forty,' and goza, 'kernel or nut.')
s.f. = cīl, 'kite.'
adj. Abounding in kites.
s.m. = ćillaṛ, q.v.
s.f. = ćilhaṛ, ćillaṛ, q.v.
v.t. To peck ( = ćikornā).
s.m. The flesh of the kite (the eating of which is said to produce madness).
s.m. Screaming like a kite; — screaming, crying; cry, noise.
s.f. Twitch, stitch; twist, sprain (cf. ćik).
adj. Bright, shining, glittering; — s.m. A rupee (in the language of brokers).
adj. Bright, shining, glittering; — s.m. A rupee (in the language of brokers).
s.f. Noise, cry, confused noise
subs: s.f. The cry of a pup; confused noise of children
s.f. Hue and cry, shout, clamour.

def: s.m. Noise, cry, outcry, &c. ( = ćil, q.v.).
s.m. A bow-string, &c.; — a period of forty days &c. = ćillā, q.v.

ety: rel. n. fr. ćihal or ćil, 'forty'
def: s.m. A period of forty days; the forty days of Lent during which the religious fraternities of the East seclude themselves in their cells or in their mosques, or remain at home fasting and engaging in divine worship; the fast of Lent; — the place of such seclusion and fasting; — quarantine; — the period of forty days after childbirth during which a woman remains unclean; — the ceremony of purification; — the period of forty days in winter when the cold is most severe
subs: ćillā kāṭnā, ćillā khaiṅćnā, v.n. To pass forty consecutive days in fasting and divine worship in a cell or mosque, &c.; to keep Lent; — to keep quarantine.

def: s.m. = ćīl, q.v.
s.m. The string of a bow; selvage, gold threads in the border of a turban, &c.; thread or string tied as a vow or a charm on a tomb or shrine or on a tree, &c.
subs: (-par), To tie a thread or string as a vow, &c. on a tomb, &c.; to make a vow
To string a bow, to bend a bow
(-par), To draw or bend a bow, to prepare for battle.

def: s.f. Scream, cry, outcry, noise, clamour.

ety: S. चिल्ल 'a kite'+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To make a noise like a kite, to scream, screech, shriek, yell, bawl, roar; to cry out, make a noise, raise an outcry; to exclaim aloud; to shriek or bawl (at, -par); — s.m. Noise, scream, cry, outcry, clamour.
wety: From Sanskrit चिल्ल (cilla) (kite (bird)) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. (f.?) lit. 'Bred in clothes,' a louse
s.m. (f.?) lit. 'Bred in clothes,' a louse
s.f. A kind of dish made of eggs; — s.m. A blockhead, a dolt, an owl; one who plays mischievous pranks.
s.f. = ćilwan, q.v.
s.m. The leather fixed to the vacant part of the tree of a saddle.
s.m. A fried cake of pulse-meal.
adj. Lousy.
s.m. A thick reduplicated coat (for soldiers), coat of mail, armour.
s.m. The apricot (syn. zard ārū; when dried it is termed kishṭā).
s.m. (dialec.), A louse ( = ćillaṛ).
s.f. Mud, mire, slush; — the turf or rushes on which the ćaras or leathern bucket rests when it is brought to the top of the well.

s.f. A curtain or hanging screen made of reeds or spht bamboos strung together horizontally at short intervals ( = ćiq); a venetian blind; a lattice; — a bamboo or a fence placed across a stream (usually with a net attached to catch fish).
s.m.(?) = ćilwan, q.v.
v.t. To cause to scream, to make (one) scream or cry out, &c.
s.m. = ćilhwāṅs, q.v.
v.n. = ćimaṭnā, q.v.

ety: = ćipaṭnā, q.v.; ćimaṭ˚ is prob. akin to Prk. चिम्म(इ) and S. चि
def: v.n. To adhere, stick, cling (to, -se); to be pasted, gummed, glued, &c.; to take in close embrace; to follow close at the heels (of), to dog, to persecute
s.f. = ćibuk, q.v.
s.m. The chin.
s.f. (?), An inferior kind of grass.
s.f. = ćimbur, q.v.

ety: by redupl. of Prk. चिम्म˚, fr. S. चि
def: adj. & s.m. Glutinous, viscous ( = ćipćipā, q.v.); — oil become viscous by age.
adj. & s.m. = ćimćimā, q.v.
s.m. A parrot; — a plant from the fibres of which coarse cloth and ropes are made.
adj. (f. -ī), Ductile, flexible, elastic; tough, infrangible, hard; tenacious.

ety: ćimṛā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ, as in ćamrāwaṭ, q.v.; — and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Toughness; tenacity; power, rigour; ductility, flexibility, elasticity.

ety: ćimṛā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ, as in ćamrāwaṭ, q.v.; — and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Toughness; tenacity; power, rigour; ductility, flexibility, elasticity.
v.n. To grow tough, become hard or stiff.
adj. Glutinous, viscous (as oil by long keeping; syn. ćimćima).
s.m. Pincers, nippers, &c. ( = ćimṭā, q.v.).
s.m. Tongs, forceps, pincers, nippers (used chiefly for taking up fire).
v.t. To cause to adhere, &c. ( = ćipṭānā, q.v.); — v.n. = ćimaṭnā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) = ćimṛā, q.v.
s.f. Pincers, &c. (dim. of ćimṭā, q.v.); — a nip, a pinch; — an ant ( = ćyūṅṭī, ćīṅṭī)
s.m. = ćinh, q.v.
s.m. China; — a kind of panic, Panicum miliaceum; — a sort of cloth
subs: s.m. Minium or red lead (it is brought in cakes or lumps from the hills to the north-west of Bengal)

def: s.f. Cry, squeak, chirp (of birds); chatter, murmur, &c.

v.n. To cry out, to squeal; to utter a cry of helplessness or humility, &c., to confess oneself beaten
ćeṅ-ćeṅ karnā, v.n. To chirrup, to chatter; to cry out, to squeal; to murmur, grumble.

ety: fr. ćī-dan, rt. Zend ćī = S. ći
def: s.m. (in comp.) Gathering, picking, collecting; — gatherer, picker, &c.; — s.f. Fold, plait, pucker, crease, wrinkle
subs: ćīn ba-jabīn honā, To have a wrinkle on the brow, to frown, knit the brow
To be covered with wrinkles or folds of fat, to become wrinkled (from fat)
To accept rebuke, to acquiesce in or submit to blame or loss.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to China, produced in China; — a Chinaman
subs: s.m. (local) 'China-nut,' the ground-nut, Arachis hypogœa (syn. mūṅg-phalī).

ety: fr. ćīdan, rt. Zend ći = S. ći
def: s.m. Millet ( = ćīnā or ćainā, q.v.).
s.m. A kind of panic or millet, Panicum miliaceum.
s.f. Pricking, smarting, burning (as prickly heat &c.); irritation, inflammation (of a member); ardor urinœ; the clap.

s.f. = ćinag, q.v.
s.f. = ćinag, q.v.

ety: for ćinaʼunī = ćinawnī, fr ćihnā, with aw inserted

ety: ćun(nā)+autī = Prk. आवतिआ = S. आपि (caus. augment)+ति+इका (i.e. आप्तिका
def: s.f. Folding, plaiting; a fold, plait; a letter; — flesh hanging from the ear of a cock; — incitement, encouragement (of troops in battle), &c. ( = ćunauṭī, q.v.); defiance, provocation; reproach, taunt, disrespect; envy, spite; an oath.
s.m. Tamarind seed (used as playthings by children).
adj. (f. -ī), Cross, peevish, fretful.
v.n. To scream, screech, squeak, squall (cf. ćeṅ-ćeṅ karnā); — to be cross, to fret, murmur, repine; to be stung, be annoyed, be irritated.
s.f. A small piece, &c. ( = ćindī, q.v.); a round piece of leather attached to a spinning wheel.
s.f. A small piece, a fragment, a scrap, a rag, a tatter

def: s.m. A chicken ( = ćingnā; ćīṅgā, q.v.).
s.m. Young (of a bird)

def: v.t. = ćigalnā, q.v.

def: s.m. A shrimp or prawn ( = ćiṅgṛā).

def: v.t. = ćigalnā and ćugnā, qq.v.

part. adj. Marked, distinguished; known, recognized

ety: ćīnh˚ = Prk. चिण्ह(इ) or चिण्हे(इ) = S. चिन्हय(ति), fr. चिन्ह
def: v.t. To know, recognize, distinguish, identify.
v.t. To cause to be distinguished, or recognized, or known.

adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to China, produced in China, Chinese; — a Chinese or Chinaman ( = ćīnā); — s.f. White or coarse sugar (cf. miṣrī, fr. miṣr); china-ware, porcelain; (hence) the face of a watch; — the Chinese language

ety: perf. part. of ćinīdan, rt. Zend ći = S. चि (चिनोति)
def: part. adj. Gathered, picked, culled, selected, chosen; select, choice.
adj. = ćīnī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. ćit.

ety: ćin˚ = Prk. चिण(इ) = S. चिनो (ति), rt. चि
def: v.t. To gather, &c. ( = ćunnā, q.v.); to build up (a wall, doorway, &c.); to enclose (a person) alive and build in, to immure; to fill (a hole) by ramming (stones, &c.) into it.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit चिणेइ (ciṇei), from Sanskrit चिनोति (cinoti). Doublet of چُنْنَا (cunnā) and related to بِینْنا (bīnnā, “to pluck”).

def: v.t. = ćīnhnā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of grain, Panicum miliaceum ( = ćīna).
v.t. See چننا ćunnā, ćinnā.
v.t. (Braj) = ćīnhnā, q.v.
s.f. Thought, consideration, reflection; attention; recollection; care, concern, anxiety, solicitude; doubt, suspense; — risk, peril, danger, fear
subs: adj. & s.m. Thoughtful, anxious; — an anxious person

ćintā-vān, adj. Thinking, thoughtful, &c. ( = ćintāʼe-mān, q.v.)
There is no cause for auxiety; it does not signify; no matter.

def: s.m. A large ant (black or red = ćyūṅṭā).

def: adj. Thoughtful, careful, full of care, anxious, concerned.
adj. Thinking (of), reflecting (on), considering, studying; caring (for), anxious (used as the last member of compounds).
s.m. Thinking, reflecting, meditating, study; anxious thought
subs: (-ko or kā), To meditate (on), to ponder, to study.

def: v.t. To cause to study or meditate.
adj. To be thought of, be considered, be meditated on; to be attended to.
v.n. To be bruised by being trodden upon.
s.f. A small ant, an ant ( = ćyūṅṭī, ćūṅṭī).
part. adj. & s.m. Thoughtful, reflective; anxious, solicitous, perplexed; — thought, reflection, care; attention; design.
s.f. Thinking, consideration, reflection.

ety: ćint˚ = Prk. चिंत(इ) or चिंते(इ) = S. चिन्तय(ति), rt. चिन्त्
def: v.t. To think, to reflect on, to study.
v.t. To pull or tear up by the roots, &c.
s.f. = ćīʼuṅṭī, q.v.
v.t. To have (a wall or a doorway) built up; to have (a person) immured, &c.
s.f. Compression; adhesion; stickiness, adhesiveness, glutinousness, &c. ( = ćipćipāhaṭ, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be stuck, to have (a thing) pasted or gummed, &c.
s.f. = H چيپڙا चीपड़ा ćīpṛā, s.m. Gum or matter of the eye ( = ćiprā, ćipṛā, qq.v.).
v.n. To stick, to adhere; to cohere; to cling (to; = ćimaṭnā)

ety: = H. पिचपिचा; fr. S पिच्च् or पिच्छ by redupl.; cf. S. पिच्छिल
def: adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, adhesive, glutinous, viscous, viscid, clammy, smeary, slimy.
s.f. Stickiness, adhesiveness, tenacity, glutinousness, viscosity, clamminess.
v.n. To stick, adhere, cohere; to be glutinous, be viscous, be clammy, &c.
s.m. The male of the ćippak, q.v.
v.t. To cause to adhere; to press (on or upon), to compress; to stick (on), to paste or gum; to get (one) into a berth, stick (a person into a place); to threaten.
s.m. = ćipkā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = ćhipnā, q.v.
s.f. The sparrow-hawk; — the goat-sucker, Caprimalgus.
adj. Compressed, flat; shallow ( = ćapṭā); drawn (as the face or cheeks), thin (also written ćippaḵẖ).
adj. & s.m. = ćippakh or ćippak, q.v.
adj. & s.f. = ćippak, q.v.
s.f. A small piece stuck on to anything, a patch (on paper, &c.); cf ćepī.

ety: S. पिच्छ+र+कः; or पिच्छिल+कः, rt. पिछ्
def: s.m. Gum; matter (discharged from the eyes; cf. ćipṛā).
s.m. (f. -ī), Gum (of the eyes = ćiprā, q.v.); — one who has a constant discharge of matter from the eyes.
s.m. = H چيپڙ चीपड़ ćīpaṛ, s.f. Gum or matter of the eye ( = ćiprā, ćipṛā, qq.v.).
adj. Having mattery eyes, blear-eyed.
s.f. See ćipṛā.
adj. (f. -ī), Sticky, viscous, glutinous, clammy.
part. adj. Stuck, &c.
(fr. ćipṭā; see ćapṭānā), v.t To cause to adhere, to stick on (anything, as a wafer, &c.); to put on (a patch); to lay down turf, to turf, to sod.
s.m. Bark; a vesture of bark; a strip of cloth, a rag, tatter; — s.f. (?) Cloth, clothes, attire; a sort of covering or dress for women
subs: s.m. Taking off clothes, denuding, stripping; violating the modesty (of).

ety: S. क्षीरी; but cf. चीर above
def: s.f. The pine, Pinus longifolia or P. gerardina or P. excelsa (also written ćīṛ, ćīl; cf. ćil-goza).
adj. Hard, stiff, tough; — unlucky, inauspicious
s.f. Offence, huff, displeasure conceived; vexation, irritation, peevishness; aversion, antipathy, dislike, abhorrence; provocation; mocking, jeer, grimace
subs: (-kī), To provoke, irritate; to mock, jeer, to banter.

def: s.m. Tearing, rending, &c.; tear, rent, slit, strip
subs: s.f. Cutting and hacking; amputation, performing a surgical operation.

def: adv. For what, why, wherefore; how.
s.m. A cock-sparrow; — the blue-throat (and prob. other small birds; cf. ćiṛigā).
s.m. A turban made of parti-coloured cloth (see bāṅdhnū)
subs: ćīre-wālā, s.m. One who wears the turban called ćīrā; a physician or ḥakīm.

def: s.m. = ćīrā, q.v.
adj. Powerful, bold, daring; uncouth, rude, uncivil.
adj. & s.m. Long, lasting a long time; existing from ancient times, old; — a long time, delay; — adv. Long, for a long time; long since ( = ćiram)
subs: (ćira+āyus), adj. & s.m. Long-lived; — a deity, a divinity
v.n. To live long, to attain to a great age
adj. & s.m. Long-lived; — a person of great age; an epithet of Vishnu and others; a crow
adj. Long-continuing or enduring, lasting, of long continuance, left for a long time
adj. Working or acting slowly, delaying, dilatory, slow, tedious
s.m. & adv. A long period; — for a long time; always; eternally; — ćir-kāl-se, adv. For a long period; long since, long ago

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Rent. torn, &c.
subs: s.m. Incision, cut, slit, wound, opening (of a wound); — virginity, maidenhead; — triangular subdivision of a field (in a survey); the pillars, &c. used to mark this subdivision
(-kā), To deflower; — ćīrā-band, ćīre-band, s.f. A virgin
part. adj. Broken, torn, ragged, rent

def: (dialec.) = ćiṛāndā; ćiṛćiṛā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of gentian, Gentiana cherayta (also written ćirātā and ćiraitā).
s.m. One pice (for a light, — used by mendicants).
s.f. = ćirāṅd, q.v.
s.m. = ćirāʼetā, q.v.
s.m. = ćirāʼetā, q.v.
v.n. To have a scanty stool.
adj. Cross, huffy, &c. ( = ćiṛćiṛā, q.v.).
adv. Long, for a long time ( = ćir, q.v.)
subs: ćiran-jīv, adj. Long-lived; — adv. phrase, Long may you live (used as a benediction)

ety: Prk. छिडाव(इ), fr. S. क्षिप्त p.p.p. of क्षिप् 'to abuse'
def: v.t. To provoke, offend, aunoy, vex, irritate; to mock, jeer, make fun of; to make grimaces at (usually with the word muṅh expressed).
v.t. To cause to tear or be torn; to cause to split, to get or have (a thing) split, &c.
The smell of burning leather, flesh, bones, or hair, &c.
adj. = ćiṛālū; ćiṛćiṛā, q.v.
The smell of burning leather, flesh, bones, or hair, &c.
adj. See s.v. ćiram.
adj. See s.v. ćiram.
adj. Old, ancient, antiquated, of long standing ( = ćiratna).
s.f. A woman, married or single, who continues to reside after maturity in her father's house; a young woman.
s.m. A slit, rent, fissure, cleft; split wood (for fuel).
s.m. = ćirāʼetā, q.v.
s.m. = ćirāʼetā, q.v.
adj. Old, ancient, antiquated, of long standing, long-lasting.
s.f. Beseeching, begging, requesting; supplication, entreaty, request.
v.t. = ćiṛānā, q.v.
s.f. = ćirāṅd, q.v.
adj. See s.v. ćira, ćir.
s.m. The plant Achyranthes aspera (said to be a remedy for snake-bites, sting of a scorpion, &c.); — a boy, child.
adj. (f. -ī), Irritable, petulant, testy, touchy, cross, snappish, peevish, fretful.
s.m. The plant Achyranthes aspera ( = ćirćirā, q.v.).
s.f. Irritability, testiness, peevishness, fretfulness.
s.f. Peevishness, irritability, fretfulness.

ety: fr. ćiṛ, redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To be irritable, or petulant, or testy, &c.; to chide, fume, fret, &c. ( = ćircirānā).
s.m. = ćirćiṭā; ćiṭćiṭā, qq.v.

s.m. A kind of grass (somewhat like young bājrā; it produces an ear like that of the kaṅgnī; and its grain, which is about the size of a barley-corn, is eaten by the poorer classes); — the thorny shrub Lycium europœum (the fruit of which is eaten, and on the branches of which goats and camels browse); — also = ćiṭćiṭa, and ćirćirā, qq.v.
s.f. = ćiṛ, q.v.
v.t. = ćiṛānā, q.v.
v.n. = ćiṛnā, q.v.
s.f. A hen sparrow; a bird
s.f. A cricket; — the hem of an under-garment.
adj. & s.m. Clothed in bark (ćīr); clothed in rags; — one who is clothed in bark, &c.

s.f. A hen-sparrow; a bird; — a kind of sewing; a gusset; — a scotch (so called from its bird-like shape); a crutch; a shuttlecock; the timber by which the column or beam and the crusher of an oil-mill are connected
subs: ćiṛiyā-wālā, s.m. (colloq.) A term of abuse
s.f. 'A golden bird'; a valuable prize.

def: s.m. Dirt, filth, ordure; matter, pus
subs: adj. Soiled with dirt, dirty, filthy, squalid; mattery.

def: adv. See s.v. ćir
s.m. = ćirk, q.v.
s.m. = ćirk, q.v.
adj. Dirty, filthy, foul; squalid; slovenly; — s.m. Ordure, dung.
s.m. A bit, piece, rag, scrap.
adj. & s.m. Ragged, in rags; — a tatterdemalion; a ragamuffin.
s.f. Smarting, tingling, pricking, smart, burn.
v.n. To smart, tingle, burn.
s.f. Wild liquorice.
s.f. Wild liquorice.

ety: ćīr˚ = Prk. चीर(इ) or चीरे(इ) = S. चीरय(ति), fr. चीर 'rag'
def: v.t. To rend, tear, slit, split, cleave, rip; to saw; cut open, to lance (a boil, &c.); — to form, create, bring into being; — to tear away, to steal
subs: (ćīr = ćīr-kar), v.t. To cleave or cut a way through, to break through (a crowd, or troops, &c.).

def: v.t. = ćīrnā, q.v.
v.n. To be irritable or huffy; to take huff or offence; to be vexed; to be easily provoked; to fume, to fret.
v.n. To be split or torn; to split; to tear; — to be or become irritated; to be irritable; to be vexed, to take offence (at, -se), cf. ćiṛnā; — to be ploughed, be tilled
s.f. The nut of the Chironjia sapida; the tree Chironjia sapida.
s.m. Red thread.

ety: rt. of ćirwā-nā+āʼī; see the preceding
def: s.f. Price paid for having wood split.
v.t. To cause to split or tear; to cause to be split or torn, to get (wood) split, to get (cloth) torn; — to split, &c. ( = ćiṛānā).
s.f. Shooting pain, throb
subs: v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb, ache ( = ćīsnā).

def: v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb, ache, smart, pain.
s.m. A riddle, an enigma (syn. pahelī).
s.m. contrac. of ćīstān, q.v.

ety: Prk. छित्तं; S. क्षिप्तं, rt. क्षिप्
def: adj. Thrown; lying flat (on the back), prostrate, supine (opp. of paṭ)
subs: (S. kshipta+anga), adj. & s.m. Lying flat, prostrate, &c. ( = ćit); — a person lying prostrate or supine
s.f. Wrestling; a kind of game, heads or tails, cross and pile
v.n. To fall or lie flat on the back, to lie prostrate or supine
v.t. To throw (an adversary) on the back (in wrestling), to discomfit, to overcome (in battle), overthrow; to win
v.n. To lie flat on the back
v.n. To be thrown on the back; to lie flat on the back, to lie prostrate, to stretch oneself; to roll over, fall down (in a swoon, &c.); to be laid low, be killed, to die.

def: adj. (dialec.); see ćit-pit.
s.f. Look, glance (cf. ćitwan).
s.f. A bit, piece, chip; a scrap, a rag
subs: v.n. To have a small piece knocked off, to be chipped, to splinter
(-meṅ), To stick a small piece (of paper, &c. on), to put a patch (on), to patch.

def: s.f. A pile of wood (on which the Hindūs burn their dead), a funeral pile, pyre
subs: (ćitā+āro˚), s.m. Ascending the funeral pile, the burning (of a widow) with the corpse of her husband
s.m. An offering of cakes, rice, milk, &c. to the manes at the time of burning a corpse
s.m. A mark where a funeral pile has been, a monument, a mausoleum.

def: s.m. Painting.
s.m. The hunting-leopard, or dog-leopard, Felis jubata; — the medicinal plant Plumbago zeylanica.
s.m. = چيتا ćītā, q.v.
part. adj. & s.m. Thought of, cared for; desired, wished for; — thought, consideration, understanding, wisdom; wish, desire.
v.t. (Braj) = ćitaunā.
s.m. A funeral pile (ćitā).
adj. & s.m. Variegated, spotted, speckled; — any spotted animal; the axis or spotted deer, Cervus axis; — a leopard; a large spotted snake.

ety: ćitā˚ = ćitāw˚ = Prk. चित्ताव(इ) or चित्तावे(इ) = S. चित्त+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To cause to observe, to draw attention (to); to apprize, inform, intimate, suggest, advise; to remind; to admonish, caution, warn; to give an alarm to, to alarm (cf. jatānā)
adj. & s.m. See s.v. cit, 'thrown.'
v.t. = ćitānā, q.v.
v.t. To gaze at, look at, see, behold.
s.f. Token, sign, clue; timely notice, caution, warning, admonition; alarm; a reminder.

ety: a dial. var. of ćūtaṛ, q.v.
def: s.m. Buttocks, &c.
subs: v.n. To support the buttocks, to sit down, to get rest.

def: s.m. A drawer of figures, a limner, a painter.
v.t. To paint.
v.t. To paint (a picture, &c.).
s.f. = ćitāʼonī, q.v.
s.m. Catchweed, the plant Cleavers, Galium.
s.m. A person who paints flowers, &c. (on wood, &c.), a painter, an artist; an engraver ( = ćitārī).
s.m. A rag, &c. ( = ćithṛā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Ragged, tattered, in rags or tatters, clothed in or covered with rags; — a ragamuffin, a tatterdemalion
subs: s.m. A pīr or saint to whom rags are offered (being stuck on a tree by the road-side); the tree on which rags are stuck as an offering to a saint; — a ragamuffin, a tatterdemalion.

ety: ćithāṛ˚ = ćithṛā, q.v.; — or ār˚ = āl = caus. augment; cf. ćithnā
def: v.t. To tear to pieces or rags ( = ćithṛe-ćithṛe kar-denā); to pull (one's character) to pieces, to treat with indignity, to vilify, revile, abuse; to fill a paper with a scrawl, to scribble.
s.f. See s.v. ćit or ćitt.
v.t. = ćithāṛnā, q.v.
